note: jsonapi_resources is a developer FRAMEWORK: it lets a module define custom JSON:API-compliant routes by declaring an ordinary route with a `_jsonapi_resource` default (a class or service id extending Drupal\jsonapi_resources\Resource\ResourceBase, with a public process() method) at a path starting with /%jsonapi%. It ships NO routes, resources, config, permissions, or Drush of its own — a bare install exposes nothing, so there is no config to plant. easy/recipe probes framework facts (the base class, how to declare a route, how the response is built) an unfamiliar agent would have to read src to answer. medium/introspection PLANTS a known custom resource route via a fixture module (jsonapi_resources_eval_fixture) so the agent must read the LIVE route table back and report the resource route's path/class; cleanup removes it (baseline = no custom resource routes). hard/execution requires the agent to actually WRITE a custom module declaring a ResourceBase subclass + a route with a _jsonapi_resource default, enable it, and is verified against the live route table + class hierarchy. All three tiers are present.

evals.prompt: In the Drupal jsonapi_resources module, I want to write a custom JSON:API endpoint. Which base class must my resource processor extend, and what one public method must it declare that the module calls to handle the request? Name them exactly.
evals.expected_output: Extend Drupal\jsonapi_resources\Resource\ResourceBase (or a subclass such as EntityResourceBase / EntityQueryResourceBase) and declare a public process(Request $request, ...) method — the module always calls process(), you cannot name a controller method.

evals.prompt: Using jsonapi_resources, how do I declare a custom JSON:API resource route in my module.routing.yml? Which route default replaces _controller, what must the route path start with, and which default lists the resource types the route may emit?
evals.expected_output: Use a `_jsonapi_resource` route default (a class FQN or service id) instead of `_controller`; the path must begin with `/%jsonapi%` (rewritten to the JSON:API base path, /jsonapi); and `_jsonapi_resource_types` lists the resource type names the route may emit (required unless the class overrides getRouteResourceTypes()).

evals.prompt: Inside a jsonapi_resources process() method, how do I turn my data into a JSON:API response? Which inherited helper builds the response, and what wrapper object do I pass it?
evals.expected_output: Build a Drupal\jsonapi\JsonApiResource\ResourceObjectData (containing ResourceObject items) and pass it to the inherited createJsonapiResponse($data, $request, ...), which returns a cache-aware ResourceResponse that JSON:API normalizes.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site (drush available) has a module defining a custom jsonapi_resources resource route (a route with a `_jsonapi_resource` default). Inspect the live route table and tell me the exact URL path that resource route is registered at.
evals.expected_output: The resource route is registered at /jsonapi/eval-fixture-info. Read from the live route table, e.g. drush php:eval iterating \Drupal::service('router.route_provider')->getAllRoutes() for the route whose _jsonapi_resource default is set (route jsonapi_resources_eval_fixture.info). Note the stored path already has %jsonapi% replaced by /jsonapi.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site a module registers a custom jsonapi_resources resource route. Inspect the live route table and tell me the exact resource processor class named in that route's `_jsonapi_resource` default.
evals.expected_output: The _jsonapi_resource default is Drupal\jsonapi_resources_eval_fixture\Resource\EvalFixtureInfo. Read from the live route's getDefault('_jsonapi_resource').

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available) the jsonapi_resources framework module is installed. Create a custom module named `jsonapi_resources_eval_build` at web/modules/custom/jsonapi_resources_eval_build that declares ONE custom JSON:API resource route: a *.routing.yml route whose path begins with /%jsonapi% and whose defaults use a `_jsonapi_resource` pointing at a resource processor class you write in src/Resource/. The class must extend Drupal\jsonapi_resources\Resource\ResourceBase (or a subclass) and declare a public process() method returning a JSON:API ResourceResponse; give the route a public access requirement (e.g. `_access: 'TRUE'`) and either a `_jsonapi_resource_types` default or an overridden getRouteResourceTypes(). Enable the module and rebuild routes/caches so the route registers without the jsonapi_resources route validator throwing. Do not just describe it — apply it to the running site. Reply with one line naming the route path you registered.
evals.expected_output: Module jsonapi_resources_eval_build is installed and the live route table contains a route owned by it whose _jsonapi_resource default resolves to a class that is a subclass of Drupal\jsonapi_resources\Resource\ResourceBase, at a path under /jsonapi. Verified against live route-table + class-hierarchy state, not the response text.
